$comment: ELF64 SHARED LIBRARY (ET_DYN, .so) for x86_64 Linux -- c150, the D-LK1-4 shared-library half (plan 14 / roadmap A1 part 1). Consumed by `src/link/format/elf.cpp` via the format-blind linker engine: `elf.type = dyn` routes to the SAME `encodeElfExecDynamic` substrate the exec image uses, with the dyn divergences (e_type=ET_DYN, e_entry=0, no PT_PHDR/PT_INTERP/.interp, base-0 VAs the loader slides, real-named .dynsym EXPORTS findable via .hash, R_X86_64_RELATIVE for internal absolute data slots, got-indirect extern data). A FOREIGN toolchain consumes the output: `gcc main.c -L. -lfoo` links against the DSS-emitted libfoo.so and the program runs. Differs from elf64-x86_64-linux-exec.format.json in: `elf.type` (dyn vs exec), NO `interpreter` (a .so is loaded by an already-running ld.so; validate() rejects one here), text `virtualAddress` = pageAlign (base-0 image by construction -- validate() pins the equality), NO `entryPoint`/`processExit`/`entryCallingConvention`/`processArgs` (a .so has no process entry; the linker synthesizes no trampoline -- the PIE-executable follow-up is exactly ET_DYN plus that entry machinery), `dataImportBinding` got-indirect (copy relocations are exec-only ELF), NO `tlsAccess` and NO tdata/tbss rows (TLS in a .so needs the general-dynamic model -- D-LK-DYN-TLS-MODEL; every thread_local fails loud at the MIR->LIR gate / linker section gate), `artifactProfiles` ["lib"], and an OPTIONAL `elf.soname` knob (absent here: per-artifact identity does not belong in a shipped format schema; DT_SONAME is emitted only when a schema declares it -- absent matches `gcc -shared` without -Wl,-soname, where the consumer's linker records the filename).
$dataModelComment: Same LP64 identity as the exec/rel siblings (64-bit Linux).
$bitFieldStrategyComment: D-CSUBSET-BITFIELD-ABI-EXACT: SysV/GNU LSB-first bit-field packing, same as the ELF siblings.
$externCallDispatchComment: Same call-site shape as the exec image: the walker synthesizes a PLT stub per extern FUNCTION (jmp through the GOT slot, GLOB_DAT eager-bound) and the call site is a plain direct `call rel32` to the stub -- pc-relative, so the code needs no fixup under the ET_DYN load slide. D-FFI-EXTERN-CALL-DISPATCH.
$dataImportBindingComment: c150 (D-LK1-4 + D-LK-EXTERN-DATA-IMPORT): extern library DATA objects bind GOT-INDIRECT in a shared library -- the walker reserves a GOT slot per imported object and emits R_X86_64_GLOB_DAT against it; ld.so writes the object's address into the slot and the c117/c149 GotIndirect lowering (lea-of-slot + deref) reads it. Copy relocations are NOT valid inside a .so (R_*_COPY is the EXECUTABLE's mechanism -- the exec owns the one canonical copy every image binds to); validate() rejects copy-relocation on a dyn schema.
$supportedDataSectionsComment: rodata (read-only, R+X PT_LOAD #1 -- reloc-FREE items only: a reloc-bearing rodata item would need a load-time write into a read-only page, D-LK-DYN-RODATA-ITEM-RELOC fails loud), data (mutable initialized globals, R+W PT_LOAD #2), bss (zero-init, NOBITS tail of PT_LOAD #2), relro (reloc-bearing CONST data, c145 -- merged into `.data` by the dynamic arm so its R_X86_64_RELATIVE slots land in writable pages, exactly why gcc places such data in .data.rel.ro). NO tdata/tbss: thread-locals in a shared library need the general-dynamic TLS model (D-LK-DYN-TLS-MODEL, not implemented); the linker's pre-walker gate rejects them loudly by this set's absence.
$artifactProfilesComment: This format SERVES the shared-library profile (plan 06 AP3). The c-subset language declares lib in its own set, so a lib-profile project may select this format; the cli profile is NOT served (a .so is not a runnable console binary).

elf.$comment: Elf64_Ehdr identity for a shared library. type = ET_DYN (3). e_entry = 0 (no process entry). class/data/osabi/machine identical to the exec sibling. pageAlign = 4096 (x86_64 Linux 4 KB pages). NO interpreter (validate() rejects one on dyn -- a .so carries no PT_INTERP). NO soname by default (see the top-level $comment). bindNow = true: eager binding, DT_FLAGS_1 = DF_1_NOW + R_X86_64_GLOB_DAT in .rela.dyn -- the same v1 stance as the exec image (lazy .rela.plt/JUMP_SLOT is D-LK6-11).

sections.$comment: Executable code. virtualAddress = 4096 = elf.pageAlign, PINNED by validate(): ET_DYN VAs are BASE-RELATIVE (the loader slides the whole image), and the walker computes baseImageVa = text.virtualAddress - pageAlign, so the equality makes the image base 0 BY CONSTRUCTION -- the gcc .so convention (page zero holds Ehdr + PHT + dynamic metadata; .text opens the second page). sh_flags = SHF_ALLOC|SHF_EXECINSTR (6).
sections.name: .text

sections.$comment: Read-only initialized data (string literals, reloc-free const globals). Folded into the R+X PT_LOAD #1 after .text (same as the exec image). virtualAddress = 0 -- computed. A reloc-BEARING const global goes to relro instead (c145) -- in a .so that distinction is LOAD-BEARING, not a hardening nicety: RELATIVE fixups must land in writable pages.
sections.name: .rodata

sections.$comment: Writable initialized data. R+W PT_LOAD #2 (W^X preserved). virtualAddress = 0 -- computed.
sections.name: .data

sections.$comment: Zero-initialized data. SHT_NOBITS (8); memsz-only tail of PT_LOAD #2. virtualAddress = 0 -- computed.
sections.name: .bss

sections.$comment: Symbol table (debugger support; the LOADER reads .dynsym, which the walker emits unconditionally on this arm). sh_entsize = 24.
sections.name: .symtab

relocations.$comment: The pc-relative kinds are the only ones legal in ET_DYN `.text` (an absolute fixup in code would need DT_TEXTREL -- D-LK-DYN-TEXT-ABS-RELOC fails loud). kind numbers match the target schema rows (the cross-reference unifier requires both sides).
relocations.name: R_X86_64_PC32

relocations.$comment: abs64 -- legal in DATA items only on this arm: the walker patches the slot with the base-relative value AND emits an R_X86_64_RELATIVE row for it (the load slide completes the address).
relocations.name: R_X86_64_64
